fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Montana Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Montana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity montana, how to transfer dietitian license to montana, dietitian compact montana, montana dietitian endorsement application

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: Regulated by the Montana Board of Medical Examiners. Recently defined distinctly through 2025 legislative sessions separating it from the generic 'Nutritionist' title.

license_types.title: Licensed Nutritionist
license_types.description: Montana has historically licensed 'Nutritionists', a title which fully incorporates the credentials and scope of a Registered Dietitian.

steps.title: Complete Education and Supervised Practice
steps.description: Complete a minimum of a bachelor's degree and supervised practice program approved by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians
steps.description: Successfully complete the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Apply for a Dietitian License
steps.description: Submit a completed application, official transcripts, verification of supervised practice, examination results, and required fee to the Montana Board of Medical Examiners.

documents.detail: Required for all applicants. Conducted via the National Practitioner Data Bank.
Must be sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
If licensed in other jurisdictions, you must arrange for official verifications to be sent to Montana.

provisional_text: Montana does not issue a temporary or provisional license for dietitians or nutritionists. You must fully meet the CDR examination requirement before being granted a license.
reciprocity_text: Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration may readily obtain a Montana license by successfully completing the application packet, submitting the NPDB self-query, and ensuring their current state licenses are verified.

faqs.question: How much does a Montana dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial cost is a $75 application fee.

faqs.question: When does the Montana license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Montana dietitian and nutritionist licenses expire biennially. The renewal fee is $100.

faqs.question: Why is it through the Board of Medical Examiners?
faqs.answer: In Montana, the regulation of dietitians and nutritionists is structured under the Board of Medical Examiners, which oversees various allied health professions to ensure public safety in medical nutrition therapy.

faqs.question: Does Montana have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Montana. To work in Montana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Montana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 5 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration may readily obtain a Montana license by successfully completing the application packet, submitting the NPDB self-query, and ensuring their current state licenses are verified.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Montana
hero.dek: Montana Dietitian License requires completing an accredited educational program, passing the Registration Examination for Dietitians, and completing supervised practice.
